# arcgisutils

`arcgisutils` is the shared foundation of the R-ArcGIS bridge. It provides authentication, EsriJSON serialization/parsing, geoprocessing service support, and portal API utilities for R packages that work with ArcGIS Location Services (ArcGIS Online and ArcGIS Enterprise).

---

## Authentication

All auth functions return an `httr2_token` object with an `arcgis_host` field attached. This token is passed to `arc_base_req()` to authorize requests.

### Auth flows

```r
# Interactive OAuth2: opens browser, prompts for code
token <- auth_code()

# Non-interactive OAuth2 client credentials
token <- auth_client(expiration = 120)  # minutes

# Static API key
token <- auth_key()

# Legacy username/password (generateToken endpoint)
token <- auth_user()

# ArcGIS Pro via arcgisbinding
token <- auth_binding()

# Shiny application OAuth2 (requires shinyOAuth package)
client <- auth_shiny()
```

All functions read credentials from environment variables by default:

| Variable | Used by |
|---|---|
| `ARCGIS_HOST` | `arc_host()` defaults to `https://www.arcgis.com` |
| `ARCGIS_CLIENT` | `auth_code()`, `auth_client()`, `auth_shiny()` |
| `ARCGIS_SECRET` | `auth_client()`, `auth_shiny()` |
| `ARCGIS_API_KEY` | `auth_key()` |
| `ARCGIS_USER` | `auth_user()` |
| `ARCGIS_PASSWORD` | `auth_user()` |
| `ARCGIS_REDIRECT_URI` | `auth_shiny()` |

### Token management

```r
# Store a token in the session (default name: "ARCGIS_TOKEN")
set_arc_token(token)

# Store multiple named tokens
set_arc_token(org_a = token_a, org_b = token_b)

# Retrieve the default token
arc_token()

# Retrieve a named token
arc_token("org_a")

# Remove tokens
unset_arc_token()
unset_arc_token(c("org_a", "org_b"))

# Validate or refresh before use
validate_or_refresh_token(token)
```

---

## Building Requests

`arc_base_req()` is the entry point for all HTTP requests. It sets the `User-Agent` header and attaches the authorization token.

```r
req <- arc_base_req(
  url   = "https://services.arcgis.com/.../FeatureServer/0",
  token = arc_token(),
  path  = c("query"),
  query = list(f = "json")
)

# Chain httr2 verbs, then perform
resp <- req |>
  httr2::req_body_form(where = "1=1", outFields = "*") |>
  httr2::req_perform()
```

ArcGIS REST API responses embed errors in the JSON body (HTTP status is always 200). Use `detect_errors()` after parsing:

```r
result <- httr2::resp_body_string(resp) |>
  RcppSimdJson::fparse() |>
  detect_errors()
```

### Pagination

Many portal endpoints use cursor-based pagination. `arc_paginate_req()` handles this automatically:

```r
req <- arc_base_req(host, token, path = c("sharing", "rest", "search"), query = c(f = "json", q = "crime"))

responses <- arc_paginate_req(
  req,
  page_size = 50,
  max_pages = Inf,
  .progress = TRUE
)
# Returns a list of httr2_response objects
```

---

## EsriJSON Serialization

Convert R spatial objects to EsriJSON for use in REST API request bodies.

### Single geometry

```r
pt <- sf::st_point(c(-122.4, 37.8))
as_esri_geometry(pt, crs = sf::st_crs(4326))
```

### Feature arrays

```r
# sf or data.frame to list of feature objects
as_features(my_sf)

# sf or sfc to JSON string of feature array
as_esri_features(my_sfc)
```

### Full featureSet (features + fields + spatialReference)

```r
# Returns a JSON string ready for use in a request body
as_featureset(my_sf)
as_esri_featureset(my_sf)  # equivalent
```

### Parsing EsriJSON

```r
# Converts featureSet JSON string to data.frame or sf
result <- parse_esri_json(json_string)
# Returns sf when geometry is present, data.frame otherwise
```

---

## Field Type Mapping

```r
# Infer Esri field definitions from a data.frame
fields <- as_fields(my_data)
# Returns data.frame with columns: name, type, alias, nullable, editable

# Create an empty prototype data.frame from a fields list
fields_as_ptype_df(fields)
```

Bidirectional type mapping:

| R type | Esri type |
|---|---|
| `double` | `esriFieldTypeDouble` |
| `integer` | `esriFieldTypeInteger` |
| `character` | `esriFieldTypeString` |
| `Date`/`POSIXct` | `esriFieldTypeDate` |
| `raw` | `esriFieldTypeBlob` |

---

## Geoprocessing Services

### Async GP job workflow

```r
# 1. Create a job object
job <- new_gp_job(
  base_url = "https://logistics.arcgis.com/arcgis/rest/services/World/ServiceAreas/GPServer/GenerateServiceAreas",
  params   = list(f = "json", travelMode = "..."),
  token    = arc_token()
)

# 2. Submit
job$start()

# 3. Poll status (makes an HTTP request each access)
job$status

# 4. Wait for completion and return results
results <- job$await(interval = 0.5, verbose = TRUE)

# 5. Cancel if needed
job$cancel()

# Reconnect to an existing job by URL
job <- gp_job_from_url("https://.../GPServer/ToolName/jobs/j1234abcd")
```

### GP parameter types

```r
# Linear distance
dist <- gp_linear_unit(distance = 100, units = "esriMeters")
as_gp_linear_unit(dist)

# Area
area <- gp_areal_unit(area = 500, units = "esriSquareMeters")
as_gp_areal_unit(area)

# Feature record set (accepts sf, data.frame, PortalItem, FeatureLayer)
as_gp_feature_record_set(my_sf)

# Record set (tabular, no geometry)
as_record_set(my_data_frame)

# Date (converts to milliseconds since epoch)
as_gp_date(Sys.time())

# Spatial reference
as_spatial_reference(sf::st_crs(4326))

# Parsing GP responses
parse_gp_feature_record_set(json)
parse_gp_linear_unit(json)
parse_gp_areal_unit(json)
parse_gp_date(json)
parse_spatial_reference(json)
```

---

## Portal API

All functions default to `host = arc_host()` and `token = arc_token()`.

### Portal self

```r
# View the portal as the current user/app
arc_portal_self()

# Authenticated user's own metadata (credits, groups, email, etc.)
arc_user_self()

# Organization's registered service URLs
arc_portal_urls()

# Federated ArcGIS Enterprise servers
arc_portal_servers()
```

### Content items

```r
# Fetch item metadata by ID
item <- arc_item("9df5e769bfe8412b8de36a2e618c7672")
# Returns a PortalItem: item$id, item$title, item$type, item$owner, ...

# Download the raw data backing an item
raw <- arc_item_data(item)
```

### Search

```r
results <- search_items(
  query      = "crime",
  item_type  = "Feature Service",
  owner      = "esri",
  tags       = c("police", "public safety"),
  sort_field = "numviews",
  sort_order = "desc",
  page_size  = 50,
  max_pages  = 3
)
# Returns a data.frame of matching items
```

### Users, groups, and content

```r
arc_user("esri_en")
arc_group("group_id")
arc_user_content("username")
arc_group_content("group_id")
arc_portal_users()
arc_portal_resources(id = "portal_id")
```

---

## Feature Collections (Web Map layers)

Build the JSON structures required to add R data to a web map as a feature collection.

```r
layer_def <- as_layer_definition(my_sf, name = "my_layer", object_id_field = "OBJECTID")
layer     <- as_layer(my_sf, name = "my_layer", title = "My Layer Title")
fc        <- as_feature_collection(layers = list(layer))
```

---

## Geometry Utilities

```r
# Convert Esri Envelope list to sf bbox
from_envelope(list(xmin = -122.4, ymin = 37.3, xmax = -122.0, ymax = 37.8,
                   spatialReference = list(wkid = 4326L)))

# Convert Esri SpatialReference list to sf CRS
from_spatial_reference(list(wkid = 4326L))
```

---

## URL Utilities

```r
# Parse an ArcGIS service URL into components
arc_url_parse("https://services.arcgisonline.com/arcgis/rest/services/USA_Topo_Maps/MapServer/0")
# Returns a list with: scheme, hostname, path, url, type ("MapServer"), layer ("0")

# Detect service type from URL
arc_url_type("https://.../FeatureServer/0")  # "FeatureServer"

# Check if a string is a URL
is_url("https://example.com")  # TRUE
```

---

## General Utilities

```r
# NULL coalescing
NULL %||% "default"

# Remove NULL list elements
compact(list(a = 1, b = NULL, c = 3))

# Coerce to tbl class (tibble-style printing without tibble dependency)
data_frame(x)

# Fetch layer metadata from a service URL
meta <- fetch_layer_metadata("https://.../FeatureServer/0", token = arc_token())
```
